name: Nemotron 3 Super
archDisplay: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE with LatentMoE and multi-token prediction, 120B total / 12B active
note: Mid-size member of the Nemotron 3 family, released between Nano Omni (April) and the Ultra 550B already in the corpus (dated 2026-06-04). Fills the size gap between Nemotron-H 56B and Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B.

results.notes: Table 5 of the arXiv technical report.
Table 5, GPQA no-tools; with-tools variant scores 82.70.
Table 5, HLE no tools; with-tools variant scores 22.82.
Table 5, 'SWE-Bench (OpenHands)' scaffold; page-32 text confirms this is the SWE-Bench Verified subset.
Table 5, 'Terminal Bench (hard subset)' - the original Terminal-Bench's hard 48-task subset, distinct from the Core 2.0 figure above.
Table 5, 'BIRD Bench' - BIRD dev set, 1,534 samples, SQLite, execution accuracy.

GDPval-AA v2, independently measured by Artificial Analysis as part of their Intelligence Index v4.1 — distinct from OpenAI's own GDPval and not previously tracked. Verified by manually aligning the model-name list against the score list on the AA model page (position 17 of 25, value 10%). [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (10%) to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale (matching the majority convention used by e.g. Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, Claude Fable 5=1932). Converted via Artificial Analysis's own documented formula, normalized=(Elo-500)/2000, i.e. raw=10*20+500=700. This formula is independently cross-validated against Kimi K2.7 Code's directly-disclosed embedded-JSON raw Elo (1186.79 for a displayed 34%, matching the formula almost exactly).]

verificationNotes:
NVIDIA's own blog gives qualitative throughput/accuracy claims (5x throughput, 2x accuracy vs prior Nemotron Super, #1 on DeepResearch Bench via AI-Q agent) rather than a specific standard-benchmark table; could not locate an independently-verified numeric score from Artificial Analysis or similar for this specific model, so no results entry is included to avoid guessing.

[2026-07-16] Checked all four missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu) against the arXiv technical report (2604.12374, both Table 4 base-model and Table 5 post-trained-model results), the HuggingFace model-card benchmark table (which mirrors Table 5), Artificial Analysis's Intelligence Index v4.1 breakdown, and NVIDIA's own launch blog; also cross-referenced sibling family members Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B and Nemotron 3 Nano Omni. Conclusion: the released/post-trained Nemotron 3 Super checkpoint tracked in this file has no published MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval score — NVIDIA's Table 5 (and the HF card) reports MMLU-Pro/AIME/LiveCodeBench in their place, matching the pattern across the whole post-Nano-2 Nemotron 3 family (Ultra 550B and Nano Omni likewise report no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval for their post-trained checkpoints). MMLU 86.01, MATH 84.84, and HumanEval 79.40 do appear in Table 4, but that table is explicitly the base/pretrained checkpoint, a different artifact from the instruct/agentic release this corpus entry represents, so those figures were not carried over. MMMU is likewise unpublished and architecturally inapplicable: this model is text-only (capabilities.vision=false, modalities=["text"]), unlike the vision-capable Nemotron 3 Nano Omni sibling which does report MMMU (70.8). All four gaps therefore resolve to omitted_no_evidence rather than a guess. (Note: a secondary SEO blog, digitalapplied.com, surfaced MMLU-Pro/GPQA/SWE-bench/LiveCodeBench figures that contradict the verified Table 5 numbers already in this file — that source was treated as unreliable and disregarded.)

[2026-07-18] Re-checked the MMMU gap per the round-22 research pass. Confirmed via the corpus file's own fields (modalities=["text"], capabilities.vision=false) that Nemotron 3 Super is architecturally text-only, per the task's explicit MMMU decision rule. Corroborated with fresh web searches across the arXiv technical report (2604.12374), NVIDIA's research/dev-blog pages, HuggingFace, OpenRouter, LM Studio, Ollama, Unsloth docs, and BenchLM — none report an MMMU score for Nemotron 3 Super, and none suggest a vision-capable variant of this specific checkpoint exists. The vision-capable family member remains Nemotron 3 Nano Omni (MMMU 70.8), a distinct model already noted in the prior verification pass. No new benchmarks were found or added this round; conclusion (omitted_no_evidence, architecturally inapplicable) stands unchanged from the 2026-07-16 note.
